metadata.description: while еџєзЎЂ: While the condition is true, the loop will repeatedly execute. The `break` statement exits the loop entirely, whereas the `continue` statement skips the rest of the current iteration and moves on to the next one. Python code example: ```python # Example using while loop with break and continue i = 0 while i < 5: i += 1 if i == 3: continue # Skip the rest of this iteration since i is 3 print(i) # This will only print 1, 2, 4, 5 print("\nUsing break:") i = 0 while i < 5: i += 1 if i == 3: break # Exit the loop entirely since i is 3 print(i) # This will only print 1 and 2 ``` This example demonstrates how `continue` skips to the next iteration of the loop, while `break` exits the loop altogether.
